title: Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse (three-prose-piece selection)
summary: The repository XML copies University of Michigan HTI item ThrnReligP, a 1997 OCR-derived encoding of only the first three prose works in the 1914 issue of George G. Perry's Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse. The issue is an enlarged and revised version whose work was completed in 1913 but whose title page is dated 1914 (for 1913); it descends from Perry's 1867 edition, while an 1889 object is an issue or reprint of the 1867 setting rather than a newly edited text. Perry transcribed all three selected works from Robert Thornton's Lincoln Cathedral MS 91 and explicitly compared only Trinity B.10.12 for Gaytryge, Arundel 288 and a printed Latin text for the Mirror, and the Vernon, Lambeth 432, and Trinity O.1.29 copies for the Abbey. Modern witness censuses are collateral: they do not retroactively become sources of Perry's or U-M's readings.
review_notes: The cataloged unit is a three-work editorial selection. Robert Thornton is a scribe and compiler, not the author named by the inherited XML AUTHOR element.
The 1913 date belongs to re-collation, enlargement, and revision work; the source issue and its title page are dated 1914 and the EETS series statement says 1914 (for 1913).
The 1889 scan retains the 1867 title-page setting and has an 1889-dated publisher backlist. It is modeled as an issue/reprint version of 1867, not a separate textual edition.

Gaytryge's modern twenty-six-witness census is preserved in the Greig entity as complete, reworked, and fragmentary carriers with historical shelfmarks and rejected false witnesses. Only Lincoln MS 91 is Perry's base; Trinity B.10.12 is an explicit comparator.

Mirror counts describe different units: Watson reports thirteen manuscripts and two early printed editions for the broad Middle English prose tradition; Goymer distinguishes ten translation versions and fully collates six complete manuscripts plus an early print; Thompson says vernacular translations and adaptations survive in at least twenty manuscripts; the current IMEP exact-title query returns twelve occurrence rows, while Wells's repertory count of twenty-three includes complete texts, extracts, and fragments. These are not silently normalized into one witness total.

The researched Mirror union includes Thornton; Vernon; Simeon/Add MS 22283; Bodley 416; Douce 25; e Mus. 232; Add MS 10053; Harley 2398 (two partial translations); Harley 4012 (fragment); CUL Ii.6.40 (interpolated); CUL Ii.6.43 (extracts/fragments); CUL Ff.2.38 (extracts); Lambeth 3597; Queen's College Oxford 324; Peniarth 395; Trinity B.14.50, O.1.74, and R.3.21; HM 502; and the 1521 Copeland/Wynkyn de Worde print. Creed extracts in Add MS 60577 and Edinburgh University MS 93, and a similar HM 501 passage, remain derivative or candidate occurrences rather than secure complete witnesses.

Johnstone says the Abbey survives in twenty-four manuscripts but tabulates twenty-five physical carriers when Stowe 39 and the Cotton end fragment attached to the Corpus 155 text are retained. That discrepancy and the difference between three named Wynkyn de Worde editions and Thompson's older five-print count remain explicit.

Complete IIIF cache means every image exposed by the provider's public object at the chosen access size, not preservation-master completeness. The Vernon and Borthwick bundles passed ZIP and inventory integrity checks.

Every cached object records target-work presence semantically: present always has an exact work_portion; absent never has one. Metadata and catalog files that merely describe a work are absent under this physical/textual test.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for ThrnReligP
entities.description: The pinned XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan HTI item ThrnReligP
entities.description: A 1997 scanned, OCR-reviewed, marked-up, proofread electronic selection containing only Perry's first three prose works. It excludes the table of contents, preface, glossary, index, and marginal glosses, retains footnotes as notes, and omits Perry's Mirror chapter numbers because they are absent from the Thornton manuscript.

entities.label: George G. Perry, Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse (1867)
entities.description: The first EETS OS 26 edition, based on Robert Thornton's Lincoln Cathedral manuscript. Its first three works and printed pp. 1-62 are the textual ancestors of the U-M selection through the revised issue.

bibliographic.citation: Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse, ed. George G. Perry, EETS Original Series 26 (London: N. Trübner & Co., 1867).
bibliographic.title: Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 26
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society

entities.label: Perry, Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse, 1889 issue/reprint
entities.description: An 1889-issued or reprinted object retaining the 1867 title-page setting and text, with a publisher backlist dated 12 September 1889. It is a carrier issue of the first edition, not evidence of a newly collated textual edition.

date_statements.label: issue/reprint
date_statements.notes: The internal title page remains dated 1867.

bibliographic.citation: George G. Perry, ed., Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse, EETS Original Series 26, 1889 issue/reprint of the 1867 setting.
bibliographic.title: Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 26

entities.label: Perry, Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse, revised 1913 and issued 1914
entities.description: The U-M source issue. Its title page says re-collated with the manuscript, enlarged and revised 1913, while publication is dated 1867, 1914 and the series statement says 1914 (for 1913). Furnivall collated through p. 18 in 1906; nine pieces were added; an unidentified collaborator freshly collated the whole; C. W. Foster checked readings; and O. T. Williams supplied a new glossary.

bibliographic.citation: Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse, ed. George G. Perry, re-collated with the MS., enlarged and revised 1913, EETS Original Series 26 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co.; Oxford: Oxford University Press, 1914 [for 1913]).
bibliographic.title: Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 26
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co.; Oxford University Press

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Perry 1867
entities.description: Complete 122-page PDF scan with complete OCR and scan metadata cached.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF, OCR text, metadata JSON, and scan-data XML

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Perry 1889 issue/reprint
entities.description: Complete 141-page Google-digitized PDF scan with OCR and scan metadata cached.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF, OCR text, metadata JSON, and scan-data XML

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Perry 1914
entities.description: Complete 164-page scan of the University of Toronto copy with OCR, item metadata, and page-number metadata cached.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF, OCR text, metadata JSON, and page-number JSON

entities.label: Lincoln Cathedral Library MS 91
entities.description: Robert Thornton's c. 1430-1450 paper miscellany and the sole base witness for all three Perry/U-M selected works: Mirror item 38 at ff. 197r-209v, Gaytryge item 47 at ff. 213v-218v, and Abbey item 59 at ff. 271r-276r.

historical_designations: shelfmark B recorded by Thomas Tanner in 1700
holding.notes: At Lincoln by 1700; current official record identifies Robert Thornton of East Newton, Yorkshire, as sole scribe.

physical_description.extent: 322 leaves surviving from an original minimum of 340
physical_description.format: Large religious, didactic, medical, and romance miscellany
notes: Nineteenth-century foliation 1-133, 133A, 134-321.
The selected items are non-contiguous and occur in manuscript order Mirror, Gaytryge, Abbey, unlike Perry's printed order.

entities.label: Lincoln catalog's sole public MS 91 image, f. 80r part
entities.description: The only image exposed by the current catalog record, a 1013 × 643 JPEG of part of f. 80r. It contains none of the three target works and is not a full-codex digital object.

entities.label: Brewer and Owen, The Thornton Manuscript, Scolar Press facsimile (1975)
entities.description: The first complete printed facsimile issue of Lincoln Cathedral MS 91, with introductions by D. S. Brewer and A. E. B. Owen. It is a consultation surrogate, not an upstream source for Perry or U-M; the current British Library record exposes a physical request route but no digitised content.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete one-volume printed manuscript facsimile with modern introductions

entities.label: Brewer and Owen, The Thornton Manuscript, revised Scolar Press facsimile issue (1977)
entities.description: The 1977 revised publication state of the complete printed Lincoln MS 91 facsimile, with revised introductions. It is a consultation surrogate, not an upstream source for Perry or U-M; the current British Library record exposes a physical request route but no digitised content. Exact image-level identity with, or changes from, the 1975 facsimile have not been verified.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete one-volume printed manuscript facsimile with revised modern introductions

entities.label: Borthwick Institute for Archives, Abp Reg 11
entities.description: Complete early Gaytryge witness at ff. 295r-297r, copied within Archbishop Thoresby's register; collateral to Perry's Lincoln text and one of the two best witnesses in modern scholarship.

physical_description.extent: 400 numbered folios plus binding matter and flyleaves exposed digitally
notes: The English target occupies public sequence positions 655-659, ff. 295r-297r; the following Latin instruction continues through f. 298v but is outside this target part.

entities.label: University of York complete public image object for Abp Reg 11
entities.description: Complete provider-exposed 893-image object cached at 1800-pixel width, including boards, flyleaves, binding matter, all published leaves, and target positions 655-659.
entities.reproduction_format: 893 color JPEG access derivatives plus source inventory

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS B.10.12
entities.description: Complete Gaytryge witness at ff. 56r-67r. Perry explicitly used an extract supplied by W. W. Skeat, noting slight differences, later linguistic features, and prose-to-verse lineation.

holding.notes: Current item-level digital access was not established in this research pass.

entities.label: British Library, Arundel MS 288
entities.description: Anglo-Norman Mirour de Seinte Eglyse witness from which Perry prints a parallel passage at p. 207 of the manuscript. It is an editorial comparator, not a proven physical exemplar of the Thornton English translation.

entities.label: Magna Bibliotheca Veterum Patrum XIII, p. 355, Latin Speculum parallel
entities.description: The printed Latin source from which Perry reproduces a parallel passage beside Arundel 288 and the Thornton Mirror. Modern research identifies multiple Latin states and retranslations, so this printed parallel is not asserted as Thornton's direct exemplar.

bibliographic.citation: Magna Bibliotheca Veterum Patrum, vol. XIII, p. 355, Latin Speculum passage as cited and reprinted by Perry.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Eng. poet. a. 1
entities.description: Late-fourteenth-century large religious miscellany containing a Mirror version at ff. 356r-359v and an Abbey version at ff. 359v-361r in the current digital foliation. Perry explicitly prints the Abbey in a four-witness conspectus; it is collateral, not his base.

physical_description.extent: viii + 342 + i surviving leaves; originally viii + 413
notes: The Mirror ends and Abbey begins together on f. 359v, IIIF canvas 605.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete facsimile of the Vernon Manuscript
entities.description: Complete 710-canvas color surrogate. Target Mirror canvases are 598-605 (ff. 356r-359v) and Abbey canvases 605-608 (ff. 359v-361r), sharing canvas 605.
entities.reproduction_format: 710 color IIIF canvases cached at 1600-pixel width

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 432
entities.description: Abbey witness at ff. 37v-68r, explicitly printed by Perry in his four-witness conspectus. A manuscript attribution to 'Richard hampspull' is spurious; Perry says the latter portion differs substantially from Thornton.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS O.1.29 (1053)
entities.description: Abbey witness at ff. 77r-101v, explicitly printed by Perry in his four-witness conspectus; Perry describes its tendency toward amplification and additions.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 22283
entities.description: Late-fourteenth-century Vernon-related miscellany containing an imperfect Mirror at ff. 163r-164v and an imperfect Abbey at ff. 165r-168r. It is collateral to Perry and demonstrates that the two works circulated together.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. e Mus. 232
entities.description: Early-fifteenth-century manuscript whose Mirror at ff. 24r-62r names Nicholas Bellew in its translator colophon and Jon Flemmyn in its copying context. Those names apply to this collateral version, not the anonymous Thornton translation.

entities.label: British Library Stowe MS 39
entities.description: Illustrated fifteenth-century devotional manuscript containing the Abbey at ff. 4r-8r. It was unknown to Consacro's census and accounts for part of the later 24-versus-25 carrier tension. An eighteenth-century John Alcock attribution is chronologically impossible.

entities.label: Pamela L. C. Greig, The 'Lay Folks' Catechism'—An Edition (2018)
entities.description: Modern critical edition using Bodleian Don.c.13 as base and recording twenty-six Gaytryge witnesses, including complete, reworked, and fragmentary copies. It is a collateral census, not an intermediary in Perry's source chain.

bibliographic.citation: Pamela L. C. Greig, The 'Lay Folks' Catechism'—An Edition (PhD thesis, University of Nottingham, 2018).
bibliographic.title: The 'Lay Folks' Catechism'—An Edition

notes: Complete: A BL Add 25006 ff. 1r-9v; C Oxford Corpus Christi 155 ff. 239r-250v; G Paris Bibliothèque Sainte-Geneviève 3390 ff. 37v-52v; H BL Harley 1022 ff. 66r-73v, acephalous from line 83; L Lincoln MS 91 ff. 213v-218v; N Nottingham WLC/LM/9 ff. 248r-256r; S Sidney Sussex 55 ff. 41r-47v; T Borthwick Abp Reg 11 ff. 295r-297r; V Trinity B.10.12 ff. 56r-67r; X BL Arundel 507 ff. 50r-54r; Z Bodleian Don.c.13 ff. 162va-165va.

Reworked: B Bodley 789 ff. 51v-68v, acephalous from line 50; E BL Harley 6615 ff. 127v-140v; La Lambeth 408 ff. 1r-17r; R Rawlinson C.288 ff. 85r-91r; U one former codex now Bodleian Eng.th.e.181 ff. 6r-10v plus Douce 274 ff. 1r-17v; W Keio MS 9, formerly Hopton Hall, ff. 3v-9v; Y Beinecke 317 ff. 21v-22r and 30v-34r.

Fragments: D CUL Dd.12.39 ff. 1v-2v; F CUL Ff.5.40 ff. 117v-119r; J CUL Ii.4.9 ff. 95v-96r; K CUL Add 6686, formerly Ashburnham 140, pp. 362-364; M BL Add 24202 ff. 35v-36v; O Chetham 60 bifolium, modern pp. 3-4; P Rawlinson C.285 ff. 61v-62r; Q Queen's College Oxford 389B fragments 24-25, formerly ff. 245 and 248 of MS 389.
Excluded false witnesses: Lincoln MS 66 (old siglum I), Trinity B.14.19 (old O), and York Minster XVI.L.12. Douce 273 is physically associated with the split U codex but carries no target text.

entities.label: Ian Cornelius, 'The Lay Folks' Catechism, Alliterative Verse, and Cursus' (2019)
entities.description: Peer-reviewed study of the 1357 commission, twenty-six-witness tradition, and form. It treats Abp Reg 11 and Don.c.13 as especially authoritative and argues that the English instruction is not securely classifiable as traditional alliterative verse.

bibliographic.citation: Ian Cornelius, 'The Lay Folks' Catechism, Alliterative Verse, and Cursus', Review of English Studies 70 (2019): 14-36, doi:10.1093/res/hgy107.
bibliographic.title: The Lay Folks' Catechism, Alliterative Verse, and Cursus

entities.label: Nicholas Watson, Middle English versions and audiences of Edmund's Speculum Religiosorum (2009)
entities.description: Current synthesis distinguishing multiple late-fourteenth-century Middle English translations. It reports about eight translations, thirteen manuscripts, and two early printed editions, and treats the three complete prose translations in Vernon, Simeon, and Thornton as independent.

bibliographic.citation: Nicholas Watson, 'Middle English Versions and Audiences of Edmund of Abingdon's Speculum Religiosorum', in Texts and Traditions of Medieval Pastoral Care (2009), doi:10.1017/9781846157691.011.
bibliographic.title: Middle English Versions and Audiences of Edmund of Abingdon's Speculum Religiosorum

entities.label: Clare Goymer, parallel-text Mirror thesis (1961)
entities.description: Parallel-text study of ten Middle English prose versions: three translated from Anglo-Norman, four from Latin, and three of mixed affiliation. Six complete manuscripts and an early printed text are fully collated; incomplete versions and the interpolated Rolle attribution are treated in appendices.

bibliographic.citation: Clare Rosemary Goymer, A Parallel Text Edition of the Middle English Prose Version(s) of the 'Mirror of St. Edmund' Based on the Known Complete Manuscripts (MA thesis, Royal Holloway, 1961).
bibliographic.title: A Parallel Text Edition of the Middle English Prose Version(s) of the 'Mirror of St. Edmund' Based on the Known Complete Manuscripts

entities.label: Index of Middle English Prose: Mirror of St Edmund title results
entities.description: Current exact-title query returning twelve occurrence rows, not twelve physical manuscripts or all known versions. It is used alongside, not as a replacement for, Watson, Goymer, Thompson, and older repertory counts.

entities.label: Boyda Johnstone, Theatre of the Mind (2010)
entities.description: Study of devotional performance in Stowe MS 39 with a detailed Abbey witness table, Stowe images and quotations, audience analysis, French-source discussion, and explicit attribution cautions.

bibliographic.citation: Boyda Johnstone, Theatre of the Mind: Devotional Performance in British Library MS Stowe 39 (MA thesis, University of Calgary, 2010).
bibliographic.title: Theatre of the Mind: Devotional Performance in British Library MS Stowe 39

notes: Listed carriers: NLW Peniarth 334A pp. 77-123; CUL Ii.4.9 ff. 69v-87r, Ll.5.18 ff. 1r-24v, Dd.11.89 ff. 2r-8v; Jesus College Q.D.4(46) ff. 146r-172r; Trinity O.1.29(1053) ff. 77r-101v; Lincoln MS 91 ff. 271r-276r; BL Add 22283 ff. 165r-168r, Add 36983 ff. 281r-297v, Egerton 3245 ff. 156v-183r, Harley 1704 ff. 37v-48r, Harley 2406 ff. 61r-83r, Harley 5272 ff. 105r-137v, Stowe 39 ff. 4r-8r; Lambeth 432 ff. 37v-68r.

Further carriers: Bodleian Douce 141 ff. 139r-145r, Douce 323 ff. 140v-159v, Eng. poet. a.1 ff. 359v-361r in current digital foliation, Laud misc. 210 ff. 136r-146v and 180r-186v (two occurrences); Oxford Corpus Christi 155 ff. 260r-268v, completed by BL Cotton Vespasian D.XIII f. 181r; Stonyhurst B.xxiii ff. 45v-60r; Keio MS 9, formerly Hopton Hall, pp. 27-43; private Bradford-Lawrence 8 ff. 1r-6v; Winchester College 33 ff. 94r-108v.

Johnstone states twenty-four manuscripts but lists twenty-five carriers; Stowe was unknown to Consacro, and the Cotton leaf is an end fragment physically supplementing the Corpus 155 occurrence rather than an independent complete copy.
Three Wynkyn de Worde editions are named: two in 1496 and one in 1500. Eighteen manuscript copies are accompanied by the Charter.

entities.label: John J. Thompson, Robert Thornton and Book-Producing in the Later Middle Ages (1983)
entities.description: Full study of Thornton's compiling and source contexts, with substantial discussion and quotation of all three target works. It reports at least twenty Mirror manuscripts and twenty-four Abbey manuscripts, while its older five-print Abbey count is retained as bibliographic history rather than silently reconciled.

bibliographic.citation: John J. Thompson, Robert Thornton and Book-Producing in the Later Middle Ages (DPhil thesis, University of York, 1983).
bibliographic.title: Robert Thornton and Book-Producing in the Later Middle Ages

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A (April 2026)
entities.description: Official U.S. Copyright Office guidance used only for the current United States public-domain term assessment of the 1867, 1889, and 1914 Perry editions.
notes: catalog_description is a provisional schema workaround for a separately delivered legal-guidance source; the circular contains no target-work text and is not used to assess provider terms, scans, foreign jurisdictions, or modern scholarship.

rights.basis: All three works and the Thornton copies predate modern copyright by centuries; this does not determine rights in the encoding, edition, or images.
The XML allows personal use, research, teaching, and linking with the availability statement, while expressly forbidding redistribution by commercial or not-for-profit content providers.
The edition was published in 1867, before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff described in U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A.
The issue/reprint was published by 1889, before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff described in U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A.

The repository says copyright Clare Rosemary Goymer and acknowledges permission to archive for study and educational use; it does not grant a general open-content license.

rights.component: IMEP database, compilation, and web presentation
rights.basis: The site credits compiled indexes and Boydell & Brewer database access but exposes no open-content license for wholesale reuse.

rights.component: Thesis, transcriptions, and embedded manuscript images
rights.basis: The Library and Archives Canada copy records a non-exclusive preservation/reproduction permission while the author retains copyright; substantial extracts or image reuse require separate review.
Public repository access does not itself grant an open-content license; author and third-party rights may apply.

rights.component: Official Circular 15A text and PDF delivery file
rights.basis: The circular is an official U.S. Copyright Office publication and is publicly downloadable, but this manifest did not verify an item-specific rights statement or separately document the statutory federal-government-work rule for the delivery file.

editorial_practices.description: Includes only Perry's first three prose pieces and excludes the other sixteen numbered works plus table of contents, preface, glossary, index, and marginal glosses.

editorial_practices.representation: Scanned and OCRed with ScanWorx, reviewed, marked up, proofread, and reviewed again; represented like the print so far as possible
editorial_practices.description: The declaration specifies the production workflow but does not claim a diplomatic manuscript transcription.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Perry's appended Latin-work chapter numbers in the Mirror
editorial_practices.representation: Omitted because absent from Lincoln MS 91
editorial_practices.description: Separates Perry's navigational intervention from manuscript content.

editorial_practices.representation: Furnivall collated through p. 18; the whole was freshly collated; Foster checked selected readings
editorial_practices.description: The responsibility for the fresh whole-volume collation is not securely named.
editorial_practices.scope: Whole edition, including all three selected works

editorial_practices.source_feature: Gaytryge appears as prose in Thornton while Trinity B.10.12 uses dots and then verse lineation
editorial_practices.representation: Printed as prose with editorial punctuation
editorial_practices.description: Perry and Skeat propose degraded alliterative verse; Cornelius's modern analysis cautions against secure traditional-verse classification.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two Latin prayers followed by full English translations in Lincoln MS 91
editorial_practices.representation: English translations printed; redundant Latin omitted
editorial_practices.description: The U-M encoding inherits this edition-layer omission.

evidence.summary: Verified 3 DIV1, 6 DIV2, 19 HEAD, 54 P, 10 NOTE1, 48 MILESTONE, 61 PB, 62 EPB, and 5 CORR, including per-work boundaries and page ranges.

evidence.summary: Stable official identifier and collection context. The exact named route returned HTTP 403 to automated requests on 2026-07-15; the pinned XML provides the verified content and header.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for religiouspieces00perrgoog
evidence.summary: Records item date 1889 and Google-derived file inventory; it does not by itself establish a new textual edition.

evidence.citation: Perry, Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse, revised 1913 and issued 1914, complete scan
evidence.locator: title page; contents; preface pp. v-xii; reissue note; printed pp. 1-62; EETS backlist
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the 1913 revision/1914 issue distinction, nineteen-item contents, first-three-work boundary, editorial collaborators, Lincoln base, explicit comparison witnesses, and EETS description as an enlarged reprint.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for religiouspiecesi00perruoft
evidence.summary: Identifies the 1914 University of Toronto scan, 164-page PDF, title metadata, and file inventory.

evidence.citation: Lincoln Cathedral MS 91, University of Lincoln Special Collections CalmView
evidence.locator: RefNo, AltRefNo, Notes, Date, items 38, 47, 59, ImageData, PhysicalDescription, Multimedia
evidence.summary: Official evidence for current and historical shelfmarks, provenance, Thornton's scribal role, c. 1430-1450 date, 322-leaf extent, exact target item folios, the complete Brewer/Owen facsimile's revised 1977 issue, and the sole f. 80r catalog image and permission statement.

evidence.citation: Lincoln Cathedral, Readers and Researchers
evidence.summary: Current appointment, written-application, academic-letter, research-hour, and digital-image or photocopy request guidance.

evidence.citation: Lincoln Cathedral, Terms of Service Agreement
evidence.summary: Current website copyright and personal-copy/extract conditions; commercial use requires license or permission.

evidence.citation: Lincoln MS 91 catalog multimedia image, f. 80r part
evidence.locator: single 1013 × 643 JPEG
evidence.summary: Confirms that the sole public image is only part of f. 80r and contains none of the three target works.

evidence.citation: Dated full-object access audit for Lincoln Cathedral MS 91
evidence.locator: catalog multimedia; page source; IIIF/manifest/digital/download route tests; targeted public search; former facsimile references
evidence.summary: No public complete codex, target-folio sequence, IIIF manifest, or download-all route was verified on 2026-07-15. The only exposed catalog multimedia is f. 80r part. Current alternatives are onsite consultation, paid reproduction, full-object enquiry, and the published Scolar Press facsimile.

evidence.locator: reference, title, scope and content, date, extent, access, and blank Digitised Content field
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the one-volume 1975 Scolar Press facsimile of Lincoln Cathedral MS 91, its Brewer/Owen introductions, current physical-item request route, and absence of a linked digitised object.

evidence.locator: reference, title, scope and content, date, extent, access, and blank Digitised Content field
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the one-volume 1977 Scolar Press facsimile of Lincoln Cathedral MS 91, its Brewer/Owen introductions, current physical-item request route, and absence of a linked digitised object; Lincoln's catalog identifies this as the revised issue.

evidence.summary: Official holding and folio-browser evidence for Abp Reg 11 and target ff. 295r-297r.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS. Eng. poet. a. 1
evidence.summary: Official date, origin, material, extent, dimensions, shelfmark, and Digital Bodleian link for the Vernon Manuscript.

evidence.locator: 710 canvases; rights; target canvases 598-608
evidence.summary: Exact source for complete public image inventory, canvas labels, image license, and target folio mapping.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection and inventory audit of Vernon canvases 598-608
evidence.locator: Mirror ff. 356r-359v, canvases 598-605; Abbey ff. 359v-361r, canvases 605-608
evidence.summary: Confirms exact collateral work boundaries, shared transition canvas 605, all 710 inventory mappings, and clean ZIP integrity.

evidence.citation: Pamela L. C. Greig, The 'Lay Folks' Catechism'—An Edition (2018)
evidence.locator: witness census and descriptions; sigla; fragments; false-witness exclusions; history of scholarship
evidence.summary: Modern critical evidence for the 1357 context and twenty-six-witness census, including complete, reworked, fragmentary, split-codex, historical-shelfmark, and excluded-false-witness distinctions. Direct automated download was blocked by WAF/CAPTCHA.

evidence.citation: Ian Cornelius, 'The Lay Folks' Catechism, Alliterative Verse, and Cursus' (2019)
evidence.summary: Peer-reviewed evidence for Gaytryge's commission, witness count, best witnesses, and caution against secure traditional-alliterative-verse classification; cached author manuscript states CC BY-NC-ND 3.0.

evidence.citation: Nicholas Watson, 'Middle English Versions and Audiences of Edmund of Abingdon's Speculum Religiosorum' (2009)
evidence.summary: Supports late-fourteenth-century Middle English activity, multiple independent versions, thirteen-manuscript/two-print scope, and the independence of Vernon, Simeon, and Thornton prose translations; full text is restricted.

evidence.citation: Clare Rosemary Goymer, A Parallel Text Edition of the Middle English Prose Version(s) of the 'Mirror of St. Edmund' (1961)
evidence.locator: repository abstract and rights note
evidence.summary: Repository abstract distinguishes ten Middle English versions, six complete manuscripts fully collated plus an early print, source-language groupings, incomplete versions, and unresolved Latin/Anglo-Norman relationships. The 132.7 MB PDF repeatedly timed out and was not cached.

evidence.citation: Index of Middle English Prose, title search for Mirror of St Edmund
evidence.summary: Current exact-title result with twelve occurrence rows; occurrence count is explicitly not treated as the complete physical-witness count.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 22283
evidence.locator: ff. 163r-164v and 165r-168r; physical description; current request route
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for the imperfect Mirror and Abbey occurrences, shelfmark, manuscript context, and request route.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS. e Mus. 232
evidence.locator: Mirror ff. 24r-62r; colophon and manuscript description
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the collateral complete Mirror, current shelfmark, and named Nicholas Bellew/Jon Flemmyn context.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Stowe MS 39
evidence.summary: Current official shelfmark and manuscript record supporting Stowe 39's physical identity and request route.

evidence.citation: Boyda Johnstone, Theatre of the Mind (2010)
evidence.locator: pp. 24-31; witness table; Stowe Abbey images and transcription; rights pages
evidence.summary: Primary modern evidence for the Abbey's French source, audience, genre, spurious ascriptions, 24-manuscript statement, 25-carrier list, three early prints, Charter association, Stowe discovery, and thesis rights.

evidence.citation: John J. Thompson, Robert Thornton and Book-Producing in the Later Middle Ages (1983)
evidence.locator: Thornton codicology; Gaytryge discussion; Mirror pp. 85-87; Abbey pp. 134-136; transcription appendices
evidence.summary: Supports Thornton's scribal and compiling role, target codicological contexts, broader Mirror and Abbey circulation, French Abbey source history, and older witness/print counts.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, Circular 15A: Duration of Copyright, April 2026.
evidence.locator: complete four-page official circular, April 2026
evidence.summary: Official legal guidance used only for the current United States term calculations and public-domain status of the 1867, 1889, and 1914 Perry publications; it does not decide provider terms, scan rights, foreign jurisdictions, or modern additions.

open_questions.question: Can Lincoln Cathedral supply or expose a complete digital surrogate of MS 91?
next_steps: Enquire for a complete-codex research reproduction, not only target folios.
If a full copy is unavailable, request ff. 197r-209v, 213v-218v, and 271r-276r with adjacent leaves.
Re-test the exact catalog, IIIF, and digital-library routes after any platform migration.

open_questions.question: Which Latin or Anglo-Norman state directly underlies the Thornton Middle English Mirror?
next_steps: Obtain and review the complete Goymer thesis and Watson chapter.
Compare Thornton against all version groups rather than assuming Perry's Arundel and Magna Bibliotheca parallels are exemplars.
Reconcile complete manuscripts, physical carriers, occurrence rows, extracts, and fragments before stating a normalized count.

open_questions.question: Why does Johnstone state twenty-four Abbey manuscripts while listing twenty-five carriers, and why does Thompson report five early prints while Johnstone names three editions?
next_steps: Consult Consacro's critical edition and Buhler's incunable study.
Separate the Cotton end fragment from the Corpus 155 occurrence, duplicate Laud occurrences, Stowe's post-Consacro discovery, and physical carrier count.
Verify bibliographic editions versus surviving print copies.

open_questions.question: Who performed the fresh whole-volume 1913 collation, and who signed the reissue note J. M.?
next_steps: Consult EETS production correspondence and council minutes.
Retain Furnivall, Foster, and Williams's explicit roles without expanding J. M. or assigning the anonymous collation.

open_questions.question: What current working route delivers the British Library's claimed full digital coverage for Add MS 22283?
next_steps: Use the current British Library request form to ask whether the legacy Digitised Manuscripts object has been restored or migrated.
If accessible, cache the complete manuscript object and map Mirror ff. 163r-164v and Abbey ff. 165r-168r.

open_questions.question: Can the complete 132.7 MB Goymer thesis be retrieved from Royal Holloway without timeout?
next_steps: Retry the repository's exact attachment endpoint later or request a copy from Royal Holloway.
Do not infer complete witness mappings from the abstract alone.
